entry.title: Atul Architecture series for SAP BTP.
entry.content:
Sharing my latest article in the Atul Architecture series for SAP BTP. This part focuses on bridging OT and IT to deliver mission‑critical grid telemetry securely into enterprise systems. Would love feedback from the community on patterns, integration approaches, and real‑world experiences.

🔗 https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-for-utilities-blog-posts/atul-architecture-3-bridging-ot-and-it-delivering-mission-critical-grid/ba-p/14409130

entry.title: Re: ABAP SAPUI5 1.71.82
entry.content: You need to invalidate the cache on the server. Check this: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/update-cached-sapui5-app-on-fiori-launchpad/qaq-p/573969

entry.title: Re: IS-U to S/4HANA utilities - Architecture in LeanIX
entry.content:
Hi Ivo, Great question. There isn’t an official LeanIX package for IS‑U or S/4 Utilities, but the standard modeling approach is:

  • Model IS‑U and S/4 Utilities as L1 applications

  • Model core modules (Billing, FI‑CA, Device Mgmt, EDM, MaCo, Move‑In/Out, CIS, etc.) as L2

  • Map Business Capabilities at L2, and optionally map scope items as L3 for fit‑to‑standard work

This structure aligns with SAP’s LeanIX guidelines for ECC/S4 and works well for Utilities programs. If you need a generic reference model, I can share a sanitized version.

entry.title: New feature of adding a version member from model is not working
entry.content: New feature of adding a version member from model is not working

entry.title: Re: Delivery Due Job log
entry.content: Try V_SA tcode.

entry.title: Re: Delivery Due Job log

entry.title: Re: Target field exposure for Substitution rule in F4406...
entry.content:
You can add Target Fields in SM34 -> View Cluster FIN_RE_CONTEXT 

Select Rule Context Id -> For JE items is FINS_ACC_JEI_1

In Structures / CDS Views select the line that has Target checked

In fields select the Write Checkbox:

entry.title: AI and Joule Upskilling Guidance for SAP Functional Consultants
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

The topics listed below are related to AI and Joule and are highly relevant to SAP Functional Consultants. Could you please share recommended learning paths, course links, blogs, or documentation that provide step-by-step guidance, configuration details, and structured learning approaches for these areas?

I am particularly interested in learning about -

Generative AI (LLMs, embeddings, and vector databases)

Agentic AI (multi-agent systems, planning, and reasoning)

AI lifecycle management

Machine Learning algorithms (supervised and unsupervised learning)

Neural networks

Natural Language Processing (NLP)

Prompt engineering

AI evaluation frameworks

Any resources, learning roadmaps, hands-on tutorials, or SAP-specific guidance that can help build knowledge and practical expertise in these topics would be greatly appreciated.

Additionally, please consider both SAP Public Cloud and Private Cloud environments when sharing resources and guidance related to the above topics.

Thank you in advance for your support and recommendations.

Best regards,
Ashish

entry.title: Re: Connection failure for TGKTST on DBW system (Host: vhpdphbddb01)
entry.content: Hello, I found a case created with SAP Support where this case was transferred to the ECS Ops Team. This is not HANA Cloud related, this is ECS. Please confirm whether the issue is fixed. Thank you

entry.title: Compliance Concern: I-9 Form Citizenship Questions in SAP
entry.content:
Hello,

We have identified that the I-9 online form within onboarding 1.0 and now 2.0 is currently prompting new hires to provide citizenship-related information through the following questions:

  • Are you a refugee, asylee, or otherwise hold a protected status?
  • Are you a citizen of one of the Freely Associated States?

After consulting with our external legal counsel, we have been advised that asking these questions is not compliant and should not be included-neither during the new hire onboarding process nor during reverification.

Have other customers raised this as a concern? Since the I-9 form is a standard SAP form and cannot be customized, have other organizations identified alternative approaches or solutions?

Note: the Paper I-9 Form does not ask these questions and that's the only workaround however, that is not a long term solution.

entry.title: Re: gCTS: parallel development on the same server
entry.content: Hi Karin, Thank you for your helpful response. It provided great insight into how gCTS operates. I wanted to let you know that I've been watching the gCTS course (DevOps for ABAP with gCTS in SAP S/4HANA) featuring you and the other SAP instructors. It has been incredibly valuable for my learning. Kind regards, Felipe

entry.title: Re: PoC Real in CNE5 w/o values PS
entry.content: Hello, I could manage to have PoC plan and real based on Time methods, not in costs. The issue is that Actual start date for WBS is not copied from the activity confirmation, so, I had to insert it manually. After that, actual PoC (based on time) is working, but I cannot solve it for costs, despite the fact I have actual posting and Settlement of networks to WBS. Thanks.

entry.title: Re: SAP Joule for Developers, ABAP AI capabilities for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2025
entry.content:
Colleagues,
Follow the steps in the instructions; this is correct.

Additionally:
Create a SR requesting to include access from your S/4HANA SR in the whitelist:

Allowlist Squid Proxy access HTTP/HTTPS(80/443): OUTBOUND to EXTERNAL

Cause:

is the lack of necessary permissions in the SAP firewalls.

entry.title: Name Format in Email Salutation
entry.content:
We are setting up the Overdue Learning and Expiration notifications, and the salutation is pulling through as: “Hello LAST NAME, FIRST NAME”

We would like this changed to either: First Name Last Name, or First Name only

How do you control or adjust the name format in notification tokens? Is this only possible through enabling General Display Name for the whole suite? 

When looking at the label, it shows as {0} {1} so from my understanding this is in first name , last name order? so not sure what to change here...

This KBA 2670640 - How to change the Name Format of users in a particular language/locale - [LMS] - SAP for Me  says that first name is {1} but this 3002163 - How to change the display order for the users' names on Learning UI - SAP for Me  says the opposite so it is not clear what order I should be putting these numbers in to ensure FIRST NAME comes first.

Also, do these label changes even matter if I bring in GENERAL DISPLAY NAME in the SF user connector?

Do the label changes and or the general display name impact the username tokens in email notifications??

Thanks

entry.title: Issue is in DME, While migrating classic to Sepa dme , format change required for remittance advice
entry.content:
While changing from classic to Sepa DME remittance advice form changes needed 

  • For France suppliers, Both the body of email and Remittance advice should be generated in French.
  • For suppliers other than France country both the email body and remittance advice should be generated in Engilsh.

entry.title: Re: Bonus Assignment Statements-Custom Fields
entry.content: @xavierlegarrec-Thanks. Whilst we are checking on the script at the statement level, could you please confirm if adding this custom field on the Business Goals file itself is a potential way forward. Will this custom field get exposed to the Bonus Assignment Statement editor by doing this?. One of the standard fields in the Business Goals file is called "Notes", is this field reportable in principle and recycled to store a String value (the same value that we need to output on the Bonus Assignment statement via a custom field)? Thanks

entry.title: New POD 2.0 Framework: Electronic Batch Records (EBR)
entry.content:
This blog post is part of a series on SAP Digital Manufacturing new POD 2.0 framework, the new generation of SAP Digital Manufacturing worker UIs.

New POD 2.0 Framework Blogs

  1. The New Generation of SAP Digital Manufacturing Worker UIs
  2. Template-Based Design Instead of POD Types
  3. Visual WYSIWYG Designer
  4. POD 2.0 Hands-on, Flexible Layout Capabilities, Part 1
  5. POD 2.0 Hands-on, Flexible Layout Capabilities, Part 2
  6. Custom UI Dialogs and Calling Production Processes
  7. Dynamic PODs, How to Dynamically Show, Hide, Enable, and Disable UI Elements
  8. Other Business Capabilities Enabled by POD 2.0
  9. Interactive Value Journey (IVJ)
  10. POD Notifications
  11. Tips and Tricks
  12. Accessing PODs 2.0
  13. Implementing Data Scan for Barcode-enabled Component Consumption
  14. Electronic Batch Records (EBR)

Introduction

An Electronic Batch Record (EBR) is a digital version of a paper batch record that captures, manages, and tracks all data related to the production process. It ensures compliance, enhances traceability, improves accuracy, and streamlines workflow in regulated industries like pharmaceuticals and food manufacturing.

In this blog post, I will show in detail how to setup and use EBR in POD 2.0.

Prerequisites

  • You have the EBR_Manage role template required to use the Electronic Batch Record API, along with the Electronic_Signature_Read, Electronic_Signature_Sign, Production_Operator, and Production_Engineer role templates required to view, edit, and refresh EBRs

    • For more information, refer to Configure Authorization

  • You've created a POD using the Electronic Batch Record Template

    • For more information, refer to Create a New POD

  • The order that's related to the batch for which you want to create an EBR for has been released using the Manage Orders app

    • For more information, refer to Releasing Orders

  • The goods receipt for the order has been posted using an Order POD

    • For more information, refer to Creating Goods Receipts

Scope

Electronic Batch Record (EBR) scope currently includes:

Create EBRs
Create and manage EBRs after or during order execution with the flexibility to add both Digital Manufacturing (DM) documents (based in MDOs) and third-party files to the EBR folder, supporting comprehensive batch records.

Review & Approve
Review, approve, and version EBRs through a robust, role-based authorization process designed to support GMP compliance requirements from the start.

Comprehensive audit trail
track actions on the EBR, providing traceability for audit and compliance purposes.

Manage EBR documents
Document-driven first release focused on flexible document management and strong authorization controls, laying the foundation for future automated data capture and reporting.

Extend and customize EBRs
Customize by leveraging the POD 2.0 framework, enabling seamless integration of additional content, workflows, and plugins as needed.

 

 

Now, let´s see how to implement it.
 
Production Process

First, we need to create a simple production process, with the following input variables:
 

To trigger 'Create Electronic Batch Records' service, with the following input mappings:
 
 
Save and deploy it.

Automatic Trigger

Next, to make the EBR creation automatic, we can use a Business Rule as trigger.

To do that, open Manage Automatic Triggers app, select Business Rules and click 'Create'.


Next, create a new Business Rule using an Event Type as trigger. For this example, I used 'Goods Receipt Request - v1'.
Then, configure the Conditions to trigger this Business Rule based on your master data scenario, including Plant, Material, etc, to trigger this event.
 

Finally, select the newly created production process as an Action Item, and map the Input Parameters as follows:
 
 
Save and deploy it.

POD 2.0

By creating a POD using the Electronic Batch Record POD Template, you can leverage its preconfigured pages and dialogs that are designed to enable, for example, regulated industries to support the composition of electronic batch records related to process and production orders on the shop floor. This includes the flexibility to add both SAP Digital Manufacturing documents as well as third party files to the EBR to compose a comprehensive batch record. Each preconfigured page and dialog comes with preconfigured plugins that organize the layout of the POD and add relevant functionality.
 
In Manage PODs 2.0 app, click 'Create',select 'Electronic Batch Record POD' template and click 'OK'.



You don´t really need to change anything here, so just click 'Save' and 'Run' (Play button) to load the newly created EBR POD.

 

Run

With all this setup in place, you can use any Order POD to post Goods Receipt for any order that matches your Business Rule Conditions, via Goods Receipt plugin.

As soon as Goods Receipt is posted, you can check Manage Production Processes app, to confirm the production process was triggered and ran successfully, creating the EBR.

At this point, you can go back to the newly created EBR POD, filter for the batch you posted Goods Receipt against, which should show on the list, so you can click to open it.

* If the EBR POD doesn´t show any data, double check your user role assignments.

Here, you can see the EBR 'Status' column, which can be one of the following:

Draft

The EBR is in the process of being created, and up to 50 documents can be added to it.

Under Review

The EBR has been created and is now undergoing a review by a reviewer who will either approve or reject it.

Approved

The EBR has been reviewed by a reviewer who has approved it.

Rejected

The EBR has been reviewed by a reviewer who has rejected it.

Released

A previously approved EBR has been reviewed again and is finally fully released as the definitive EBR for the batch. At this point, an EBR Summary Report can be generated if the Automatically Generate EBR Report property of the EBR Table plugin is enabled. For more information, refer to: EBR Table

Once you click to open the batch, EBR POD should open the EBR created and display 'General Information' for the selected batch.

Then, click 'Documents' tab and then 'Add' button, to upload any file documents related to this batch.

It's important to know that the documents you add to an EBR aren't treated as personal information or sensitive personal information by the system. Ensure that you don't use personal information or sensitive personal information when adding documents to EBRs.

After uploading all the batch related documents, you can click 'Complete', to update the status of the EBR to 'Under Review' and initiate the EBR review process, which will require an Electronic Signature.

The EBR POD review and approval flow will control the status flow of the EBR via specific authorization roles to ensure at least 4-eyes principle review.

On the right-hand side under 'Activity Log', you can see the updated status of the EBR, which is now 'Under Review'.

During the review process, the EBR can be either Approved or Rejected. To move on, click 'Approve', which will again require an Electronic Signature.

On the right-hand side under 'Activity Log', you can see the updated status of the EBR, which is now 'Approved'.

If Rejected, users can either 'Create New Version', or 'Cancel and Generate EBR Summary Report'.

If a New Version is created, the review process can carry on using the new version, and the older rejected versions remain available in view mode.

In case of 'Cancel and Generate EBR Summary Report', the EBR is cancelled and an EBR_Summary_Report_000BATCH.pdf is created.

Once Approved, click 'Release and Generate EBR Summary Report' to move on, which will again require an Electronic Signature.

On the right-hand side under 'Activity Log', you can see the updated status of the EBR, which is now 'Released'.

Click 'Documents' tab, to see the EBR_Summary_Report_00000BATCH.pdf that is automatically generated, and click 'Preview' to view it, you can also check the checkbox and click 'Download Documents' to download them.

Preview the EBR Summary Report.

Learn more at about Electronic Batch Record (EBR) at:

  • Electronic Batch Record POD Template
  • Create an Electronic Batch Record
  • API

Conclusion

Electronic Batch Records are a key capability for manufacturers operating in regulated industries, helping ensure compliance, traceability, data integrity, and audit readiness throughout the production lifecycle. With the new POD 2.0 framework in SAP Digital Manufacturing, organizations can rapidly deploy a modern and intuitive EBR experience while leveraging built-in workflows for document management, electronic signatures, review and approval processes, audit trails, and automated report generation.

As demonstrated in this blog post, the implementation of EBRs can be streamlined by combining Production Processes, Automatic Triggers, and the preconfigured Electronic Batch Record POD Template. This allows EBR creation to become an integrated part of the manufacturing execution process, reducing manual effort while improving consistency and compliance.

Beyond the current document-centric capabilities, the extensibility of POD 2.0 and the planned future enhancements around automated data capture and report generation provide a strong foundation for increasingly digital and paperless manufacturing operations.

I hope this blog has helped you understand how to configure and use Electronic Batch Records in SAP Digital Manufacturing. Stay tuned for more POD 2.0 blogs, where we will continue exploring the powerful capabilities enabled by the next generation of SAP Digital Manufacturing worker UIs.

 

Do you like this post? Please let me know in the comments section what you think. Any feedback is highly appreciated.
Or, if you have any questions, please check SAP Community Q&A Area, or comment down below.

Thanks,
Manoel Costa

entry.title: Re: PoC Real in CNE5 w/o values PS
entry.content: Try rescheduling the project utilizing strict, bottom up scheduling, and where network activity sets the dates on the WBS

entry.title: Re: Difference of advanced foreign currency valuation and...
entry.content:
Hello Divyesh, thank you for your answer. Do you know if this feature will be included in the advanced valuation in the future?

And when will the classical valuation be deprecated? SAP always tells "in a future release" - but when exactly? We need to know this in order to determine whether it still makes sense to discontinue the traditional evaluation

Furthermore we would now have a solution for debitors and creditors. But do you have any ideas or a workaround for general ledger accounts where the balance fluctuates (i.e., sometimes positive, sometimes negative)?
I would be thankful for any advice.

Kind regards

Fabian

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA System Conversion: Reduce the memory footprint of Finance obsolete tables
entry.content:
Great, insightful blog! 
Similar for Sales, Procurement meaningful?

entry.title: Re: CSapEditiorCtrl::GetObject: Object 164 does not exist...
entry.content:
This issue occurs because the SAPscript editor is no longer part of the SAP GUI default installation selections

You need to download it separately. 

The Installer for SAPscript Legacy Text Editor can be downloaded from SAP Software Download Center:

For GUI 8.00:

https://me.sap.com/softwarecenter

-> Support Packages and Patches
-> Click on tile 'Software Downloads' which starts the Lauchpad
-> In the header select 'SUPPORT PACKAGES & PATCHES'
-> Select 'By Category'
-> SAP Frontend Components
-> SAPSCRIPT LEGACY TEXT EDITOR
-> SCR LEGACY TEXT EDITOR GUI8.00

The downloaded installer can be installed locally or can be included to SAP GUI Installation Server for installation and centralized distributed.

The extending of the existing SAP GUI Installation Server Configuration can be done by following steps:

- Download 'SAPSCRIPT LEGACY TEXT EDITOR' from SAP Software Download Center
- Extract the installer’s content in one folder, by calling the installer file from command line with the option /extract=”<FOLDER_NAME>”  
     e.g. SCRLTESP76000_0-80004481.EXE /extract=”SAPscriptEditor”
  (This example will create a folder named ”SAPscriptEditor” and extract the installer’s content into it.)
- Start NWSAPSetupAdmin.exe from the SETUP directory of the installation server
- Push the Import Products in toolbar
- Follow the wizard and supply the path to the source containing the extracted product to be added to the installation server.
    e.g. SAPscriptEditor/Setup

SAPscript Legacy Text Editor is an obsolete component and is replaced by "MS Word as SAPScript Text Editor". 
More information about "MS Word as SAPScript Text Editor" you can in the note 742662.

entry.title: Inconsistence in payroll results (tables C1/RT)
entry.content:
Has anyone encountered the following error during an off-cycle posting?

I found a few KBAs that appear to be related, but I'm looking for a more detailed explanation of how to troubleshoot and resolve this issue, particularly when the Payroll Control Record has already been exited.

Any guidance on the checks that should be performed and the appropriate corrective actions would be greatly appreciated.

 

Inconsistence in payroll results (tables  C1/RT)

Message No. 3G101

Diagnosis

An inconsistency results has been found durin

entry.title: Sostenimiento de la Solucion de Nube / Sustaining your Cloud Solution
entry.content:
Únete a nuestra sesión de Sostenimiento de tu Solución en la Nube y descubre las mejores prácticas para mantener y optimizar tu entorno SAP SuccessFactors.

Hablaremos sobre estrategias clave de soporte, compartiremos consejos prácticos para abordar desafíos comunes y alinearnos con los objetivos estratégicos de tu organización.

Esta sesión está diseñada para equipos de RH, TI, administradores de sistemas y líderes de negocio que deseen fortalecer su enfoque en la gestión eficiente de sus soluciones SAP en la nube.

 

¡No te lo pierdas!

entry.title: Change Management - Steps to help drive Adoption and Value Realisation from SuccessFactors HCM
entry.content:
Overview

Creating a successful change management strategy requires addressing key business considerations and decisions that align all stakeholders with a unified vision. By collaborating on this process, you can develop your initial program documentation while identifying any gaps and areas needing further exploration.

 

Join us for an interactive webinar where Transformation Advisor Nicole Wright will share real-world examples and leading practices from various industries. This is a unique opportunity to engage with peers, ask questions, and learn from the experiences of professionals across different organizations

 

Who Should Attend

HR and IT Business Leads

Project Sponsors

Change Manager

Project Manager

Super Administrators

HR and IT Functional Leads

SME

 

We will talk about

Program Design

Persona Development 

Communication 

Training Planning 

Sustainment & Reinforcement

SAP Change Management resources

entry.title: Let's Talk: Sustaining your Cloud Solution
entry.content: Come prepared to engage in an interactive discussion between SuccessFactors customers and SAP experts, where we will explore ideas for establishing a dedicated team with clearly defined roles and responsibilities to ensure your Human Capital Management (HCM) solution stays relevant and effective."

entry.title: Steps to shape the ideal sustainment model to get the best results from SuccessFactors HXM
entry.content:
Managing an HR tech ecosystem can feel like wrestling an unruly beast. Multiple modules, siloed teams, and unclear governance lead to headaches. Take back control with proven Transformation Programs. Introducing 1:Few Sessions for SAP SuccessFactors customers.

 

In this interactive webinar, Transformation Advisor Nicole Wright, together with SAP SuccessFactors  Release Management Expert and SAP HR guest speaker will share real-world examples and leading practices  to different customers where you can share, ask and learn from your colleagues in other organizations

 

Who Should Attend

HR and IT Business Leads

Project Sponsors

Change Manager

Project Manager

Super Administrators

HR and IT Functional Leads

SME

 

We will talk about

Relevant programs to be successful at your transformation
• Agile governance models to streamline cloud solutions

• Release Management
• Clear roles and responsibilities for HR, IT, and other stakeholders
• Setting up Service Delivery Model to support the sustainment of SuccessFactors

• Setting up Operating Model to support the sustainment of SuccessFactors
• SF Admin Capabilities


 

We look forward to having you attend the event!

entry.title: Re: 599803/2026 follow up...unable to determine how to im...
entry.content:
Hello,
I am not sure was able to understand the requirement. However, for implementing custom logic in UKM_FILL, inside method IF_EX_UKM_FILL~FILL_FIELDS, you could change IS_NOTIFICATION-DATE and the date inside IS_NOTIFICATION-OBJKEY. Then add the entry in IS_NOTIFICATION to Et_xi_msg. That way, the credit exposure should be updated with the changed value.
You may check the method IF_EX_UKM_FILL~FILL_FIELDS in example class CL_EXM_IM_UKM_FILL to get an idea on how we can loop through IS_NOTIFICATION, change a value if needed and then pass it to et_xi_msg.

Best regards.

entry.title: Let's Talk: Efficient Data Management
entry.content: Prepare to engage in an interactive discussion between SuccessFactors customers and SAP experts, where we will explore ideas managing data in an effective way

entry.title: Adopción y Activación de IA - Lo mejor de la IA de SuccessFactors
entry.content:
Adopción y Activación de IA – Sacando el Máximo Partido a SuccessFactors AI

Acompáñenos en una discusión interactiva que reunirá a clientes de SuccessFactors y a expertos de SAP en IA y transformación, para explorar qué impulsa realmente el éxito de la IA en 2026, yendo más allá de la simple activación.

Sesión Dos: Impulsando la Adopción, el Valor de Negocio y el Comportamiento de las Personas

En esta sesión abordaremos:

  • Un repaso de los factores que impulsan la adopción
  • Enfoque en experiencia y habilitación
  • Gestión del cambio y estrategia en acción
  • Medición de la adopción y del valor de negocio
  • Ejemplos prácticos de lo que ha funcionado en distintas organizaciones

Esta sesión está diseñada para ayudarle a convertir la estrategia en acción, impulsando cambios de comportamiento significativos y logrando un valor de negocio medible a partir de sus iniciativas.

entry.title: Re: Jonys Arcanjo
entry.content: Dear Jonys, Do you have an email or write to me pls to m.calzada.de.neumann@sap.com looking forward!

entry.title: Re: Configuration for Automatic HU Packing during GR for...
entry.content:
Good day @kkumara 

Thank you for your question.

For automatic Hu packing during Goods Receipt from a Production Order, the relevant standard scenario is Warehouse Production Integration with Synchronous Goods Receipt.

In simple terms you need to know the following:

  • Automatic Hu packing is not controlled only from the material master.
  • The production GR must be relevant to warehouse.
  • The storage location must be assigned to Warehouse Management.
  • You must use packing instructions or the supported automatic packing setup to maintain the packing logic.
  • If the warehouse production integration scope is not active, automatic HU packing during production GR will not work.

The main configuration and master data check you need to do is as follows:

  1. Check whether warehouse production integration with synchronous goods receipt is active.
  2. Check whether automatic goods receipt is active through the production scheduling profile or operation control key.
  3. The production order GR storage location must be the warehouse managed storage location mapped to the warehouse. If the order receives into a non warehouse managed storage location, HU packing in warehouse processing will not be triggered.
  4. Maintain the packing instruction or automatic packing rule for the finished product and packaging material. This ensures the system know how the finished product is packed into HU.
  5. Make sure the finished product and packaging material are extended to the required plant and warehouse related views where applicable.

Have a look at these references for more guidance.

https://learning.sap.com/courses/implementing-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition-warehouse-management/integrating-warehouse-production-with-synchronous-goods-receipt_ab633927-d2f1-4ce2-8de7-7d9fa6581f05?

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/2bba750d1e124e1ea2a039bb1cd9b6c5/e003b753128eb44ce10000000a174cb4.html?locale=en-US

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: Creating Customized Internal Order Report, which prov...
entry.content:
You have involved so heavy tables which are creating records almost for every transaction happens in SAP system, need to reduce the table or split the program into separate programs - 

  1. Fetch the internal order created during week
  2. fetch the budgeting for newly created IOs
  3. take the output of programs.

This is so helpful !!

If that is the case revert it back to P&L.

 

thanks

Good day all!

Is it a way to reverse an ARM to correct the GR posting date? The ARM es already completed, but the GR posting date needs to be adjusted (user didn´t correct the date before posting). My understanding is that the ARM can´t be changed anymore. Is there any way to do it in the ARM? Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Re: Hello SAP Professionals,I would appreciate your guida...
entry.content: Hi @darshsk  - I am a SAP MM consultant, and I can see there are huge number of resources available and product is quite mature, I would recommend you to go for PM and then later add EPPM in your portfolio.

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 07 - Authorization annotations
Re: How to Implement SAP Business One projects successfully
entry.content:
Great breakdown on the implementation steps. Having run 20+ SMB go-lives in the Hyderabad market, I'd add one critical nuance: the go-live week is where Hyderabad SMBs lose the most money, and it's almost never a technical issue — it's a data readiness and visibility problem.

Three failure patterns I see repeatedly:

1. Master data validation is skipped under time pressure.
Teams migrate from Tally/legacy spreadsheets and the item master is "good enough" on cutover night. Three weeks later, inventory reports are useless because unit of measure conversions weren't standardized. The fix: run a parallel inventory count in the old system AND SAP B1 for 2 weeks before go-live. Any variance >2% stops go-live until resolved. Without clean master data, you have no real-time visibility — you're just moving the same bad data into a new system.

2. User training is treated as a checkbox.
SMB owners assume the consultant will "handle it." What actually happens: 3 PowerPoint slides, a rushed demo, and a team that can log in but can't process a goods receipt. The result: workarounds that corrupt data, followed by 6 months of cleanup. Better model: identify 2–3 power users per department in week 1. Train them deeply. They become your go-live support army.

3. No cutover rollback plan.
If something breaks on go-live night, what's the decision tree? Who decides to rollback vs. push through? This sounds obvious but almost no SMB implementation documents this. The result: 48 hours of chaos before the inevitable rollback — which costs more than if you'd planned for it.

The go-live checklist I use for every Hyderabad implementation:

  • Parallel run confirmation (both systems running simultaneously for at least 5 business days)
  • Rollback decision criteria — documented, with named decision owner
  • Data freeze window (no master data changes 48 hours before cutover)
  • Go-live support team: internal champion + consultant available for 72 hours straight
  • First-week close check: finance runs month-end in SAP B1 within 5 business days of go-live
  • Batch cost gap analysis: For manufacturing and pharma clients, we now run an AI-assisted batch cost variance check in the first week — catches data migration gaps before they compound into month-end surprises

The single best investment: A formal 3-day parallel run before go-live where the team processes every real transaction in BOTH systems simultaneously. Painful? Yes. Expensive? Yes. Cheaper than data recovery after a bad cutover? Far less.

The teams that come to go-live with clean, validated data and real-time visibility dashboards ready from Day 1 close their first month-end in 3–4 days. Teams that skip this average 3–4 weeks of post-go-live disruption.

What specific module are you struggling with most during go-live? Happy to go deeper on any of these.

— Venkata Siva Reddy Polu, TEKROI
SAP B1 implementation for Hyderabad pharma and manufacturing SMBs | TekAI: AI-powered operations intelligence on SAP B1

entry.title: Re: New SAP Activate Roadmap for SAP Sales Cloud V2
entry.content:
Hello @YashikaYTD ,

We are currently working on releasing the Service Cloud V2 and ESM Roadmaps. Stay tuned for the release announcement.

entry.title: Re: Roberta Fernandes
entry.content: She is excellent....

entry.title: F3029 - Quick Create Memo Records
entry.content:

Nickerson

entry.title: Re: For CDC-enabled CDS Extraction replicated to AWS s3,...
entry.content:
Hi, there should be an empty row with keys only flagged with "is_deleted" field. 

For Parquet as target.

entry.title: Re: Error "Space Version of space <SPACEID> is too low to run transformation flow" i
entry.content:
yes, ... sometimes it is difficult 🙂 

If you have CLI, try to re-deploy all Transformation Flows via script at once, if not, you can collect all Transformation flows into a package directly in PROD and re-deploy the package in PROD again. 

Once I raised an OSS stating, the BW Technical Content does not provide simple Process Chain overview as described in the documentation (not ST13). The solution was that the documentation was adjusted and I could develop the functionality on my own 🙂

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 01 - Common types and aspects
entry.content: 3f3bab32a67185c0b6a65de8dc72c75145545d0959afd5fa78492fdfdc96df4b

entry.title: Re: Bad Data Product, Bad AI - Now What?
entry.content:
I would say the AI Model. So the Architect with Data Engineer? Product Owner should be involved as well. 

Data Products can be flawed. The kind-of-support driven approach. Quickly finish and go for next, when it breaks be faaaar away :). Product owner, business accepts solutions even if not fully tested and even with flaws. Partially no time to test, providing least experienced for testing and not really knowing what is wanted. No AI as of now can provide the content. Therefore the experience matters, but this is difficult explain to cost-driven-management :). 

So, more or less, product owner accepted, but the Data Engineering Team must expect flawed sources and must ensure proper testing of the model I would say,.

entry.title: Re: Teaching AI Agents Best Practices: A Skills Workspace for CAP Development
entry.content:
Hi Daniel,

thanks for this interesting post! When developing with AI and especially when starting with a requirement description turning this into a functional specification and further more into a technical specification, you have to provide the LLM the corresponding context. Like architectual landscape descriptions, available technologies and tools. ADR's (Architectural Decision Record) are also mentioned often in that context. Do you have any experience in using this for SAP developments or do you know if SAP is working on a best-practices guide, like Spec Driven Development in SAP context? 

Regards Stefan

entry.title: Re: Outbound Delivery blocked due to TM status - ASR
entry.content: Try implementing following note, it helped me --> 3734291 - Ready for Warehouse Processing fails in Adv. SR Stock Transport Order Process

entry.title: Re: System based assessment issue on Submitting SAP Certi...

I made a new attempt hopping they've fixed the problem but the result was the same, as of today my ticket is still "In Process" with no response yet. In my ticket regarding that last attempt I attached screenshots with all the code as prove that the tasks were completed. But as there is no response yet.

entry.title: Re: Unboxing SAP ABAP Tools for VS Code + MCP + a small announcement
entry.content:
Hi Mattias,

very interesting, thanks for that. There are MCPs for CAP, Fiori Elements, UI5 and MobileDev. My collegues told me that they work pretty well. But there's nothing useful on the ABAP side. So i'm glad you made a step in this direction and provided the git-repo. I'll have a look at it for sure. Do you have any experience with spec driven development in SAP context? we have some SDD experience but mainly in the non-sap field. SAP is different since you have a vast technology park and standard-elements like CDS, API, ODATA and more, which you would like to integrate in the context somehow.

Regards

Stefan

Here is the link to it: SAP Certified - SAP Incentive Management

Thanks for your interest in this exam!

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 07 - Authorization annotations
Request for Instructions to Verify PaPM Application Users in Production and Development
entry.content:
Hello Team,

I would like to request assistance on how to check how many users are using the SAP PaPM application in both the Production and Development environments.

Could you please provide step-by-step instructions on how to verify:

  • The number of users who have access to PaPM, and
  • The number of users who are actively using the PaPM application?

This information will help us understand usage patterns across environments and support our operational needs.

Thank you for your help.
Please let me know if any additional details are needed.

Kind regards,
Sarfaraz

entry.title: Re: LIST_TO_ASCI no longer works with ABAPLIST in EHP7
entry.content:
Yes in old release you could pass a table with structure ABAPLIST but new release will need listasci to be pure character type table.

To try with minimum changes, fix only the declaration :

DATA: v_asci TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF string.

entry.title: Re: Call for Registration for SAP INSIDE TRACK Columbus 2026 - 25th July 2026
entry.content: Please register for this event and make it a big success.

entry.title: Possible issue in Alternative Account Determination after renumbering YCOA
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

We have recently performed a renumbering of the YCOA  in our SAP S/4HANA Cloud system.

My concern is related to the impact this renumbering might have on the Alternative Account Determination.

As you can see in the screenshot below, the accounts have changed from their original numbers to new ones after the renumbering:

Will the existing configuration in Alternative Account Determination still work as expected with the new account numbers?

Do we need to review or adjust the mappings manually after the renumbering process?

When going into the Manage Chart of Accounts app and opening YCPT (which in our case is the alternative chart of accounts for Portugal), the list appears empty. However, when navigating to G/L Account Master Data, the accounts are there.

Two questions from this:

  1. If we post normal journal entries using the primary YCOA and then filter by alternative account, could we run into issues given that YCPT appears empty in the Manage Chart of Accounts app?
  2. When running statutory reports such as SAF-T, financial statements, VAT, and balance sheets - could the alternative account mapping cause problems here, or will the reports still work correctly regardless of YCPT appearing empty in that view?

Any insights or experiences on this topic would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks in advance!

I have an app that runs only for a test manager credential or would appreciate the ability to toggle the connection for it.

entry.title: Re: Feature request - @sap/ux-ui5-tooling - fiori-tools-proxy -> Add credentials with env support
entry.content: Is this still working in 2026? or has it been replaced with SAP connection manager entirely?

entry.title: How to Configure Asset Disposal Posting (French Requirement – Separate NBV and Sales Revenue)
entry.content:
Hello,

We are currently implementing SAP for our subsidiary in France and have a question regarding the accounting treatment for fixed asset selling.
Under French accounting rules, when a fixed asset is disposed of, it is required to post:

The selling of the asset (acquisition cost and accumulated depreciation)
The net book value (NBV)
The sales amount (revenue)

These need to be recognized separately.
For example:

Acquisition cost: 1,000 EUR
Accumulated depreciation: 700 EUR
Net book value (NBV): 300 EUR
Sales price: 500 EUR

The expected accounting entries are:
1) Asset retirement
Dr Accumulated Depreciation 700
Dr Net Book Value (Expense) 300
Cr Acquisition Cost 1,000

2) Asset sale
Dr Accounts Receivable 500
Cr Sales price 500

(Then the gain/loss is reflected separately through the NBV and revenue accounts.)
However, we are not sure how to configure SAP (FI-AA) to achieve this type of posting, especially:

Separating NBV and sales revenue (instead of directly posting gain/loss)
Posting acquisition cost and accumulated depreciation correctly at the same time

Could you please advise:

Which configuration settings are required (e.g., account determination, transaction types)?
Whether this scenario can be handled using standard SAP functionality?
If any specific transaction types or posting variants should be used?

Any guidance or examples would be greatly appreciated.
Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Re: Bonus Assignment Statements-Custom Fields
entry.content: Neither me or our custom reporting expert can confirm this as it is basically repurposing a standard field for your design which we have never tried before and do not recommend. .

entry.title: Re: selection in practice mode does not work the first time
entry.content:
Hi Eric,

This is unusual behavior so something is not correct.  I can think of one thing that is worth checking before you should raise a ticket to get support involved.

If you open one of the Simulations causing an issue in Producer and click on an action macro that does not play correctly in Practice Mode, is the Action value set to Left Click or something else?  It should be set to Left Click if you want it to behave normally.

 

 

If the value is set to Left Click but you are still getting the errors, please raise a support ticket using Component ID KM-SEN-PRO and have support take a look.

Regards
Shane

entry.title: Cloud ALM - personal data
entry.content:
I need to know what personal data is collected by the Cloud ALM apps stated in KBA DIGITS. These are:

  • Integration Monitoring 
  • Synthetic User Monitoring
  • Real User Monitoring
  • Configuration & Security Analysis
  • Operation Automation
  • Change and Deployment Management
  • Job & Automation Monitoring
  • Intelligent Event Processing
  • Business Process Monitoring

Our compliance team needs this information, and I could not find this in the documentation for all apps.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Cloud ALM - personal data
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

Thanks for contacting SAP Support.
I checked your description, it seemed that you have found the overview KBA ##3429058 already. But for the detailed information about personal data collection for each application, please kindly hear from experts of each different component by separate cases.
For example, if you want to check personal data collection about Change and Deployment Management, please check with component SV-CLM-IMP-FTR.

Relevant components are as below:
 

Integration Monitoring                                  SV-CLM-OP-IM

Synthetic User Monitoring                            SV-CLM-OP-SUM

Real User Monitoring                                     SV-CLM-OP-RUM    

Configuration & Security Analysis               SV-CLM-OP-CSA

Operation Automation                                  SV-CLM-OP

Change and Deployment Management     SV-CLM-IMP-FTR

Job & Automation Monitoring                     SV-CLM-OP-JM

Intelligent Event Processing                         SV-CLM-OP-IEP  

Business Process Monitoring                       SV-CLM-OP-BPM   

 

##3429058 - Data Transfer to SAP Cloud ALM

 

Best regards,

Haterm
SAP Support

entry.title: Our RISE systems are not visible in our SAP Cloud ALM RISE dashbord
entry.content:
Our systems from GTS landscape are not visible in Cloud ALM RISE with SAP methodology dashboards. Systems are visible in Landscape Management and monitoring is working.
However, I can't see option to add them manually to dashboard scope.

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Our RISE systems are not visible in our SAP Cloud ALM...
entry.content:
In RISE with SAP Methodology Dashboard, only the SAP S/4HANA or SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition systems with software component S4CORE are supported. 
All other system type like SAP ERP, SAP S/4HANA Foundation are not supported.

  • Under the System View, only the Production, Test, and Development system type are visible. A demo S/4HANA system will not be displayed.
  • Under the Operations View, only those productive clients associated with the productive systems (technical) that appear in the System View can be displayed.

For more details, please review the following SAP help portal guide:

  • https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-alm/applicationhelp/faq-system-view
  • https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-alm/applicationhelp/faq-operations-view
  • https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-alm/applicationhelp/system-view
  • https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-alm/applicationhelp/operations-view

entry.title: Re: Unable to export cost component split in Display Actu...
entry.content: I cannot find the CDS view 'I_ActualCostComponetSplit' and 'I_CostComponentSplit'

entry.title: "Post Revaluation" Function Availability & Documentation Gap
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

We are reaching out because we have received guidance from SAP OSS that conflicts with what we observe in our system, and we have been unable to find any official documentation to clarify the situation. We would greatly appreciate input from community members who may have encountered the same issue.

Issue 1: "Post Revaluation" function stated as currently unavailable We received the following response from SAP OSS: "What I mean is that 'Post Revaluation' is not yet available and will be a functional improvement in the future. If you want to do the Revaluation Posting, please use the 'Other Transactions' option."

The "Post Revaluation" menu item is visible in our Fixed Asset Management menu in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. We expected this menu item to be a fully functional feature. However, OSS has informed us that it is not yet available and is planned as a future enhancement. This is unexpected, as the menu item is present in the system without any visible restriction or warning.

Issue 2: No documentation found to support this restriction We have searched SAP Help Portal, SAP Notes, and KBA databases, but we cannot find any official documentation that:

  • States that "Post Revaluation" is currently unavailable or restricted in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • Confirms that "Other Transactions" is the officially recommended workaround for revaluation posting
  • Provides a planned release timeline for when "Post Revaluation" will become fully functional

The absence of documentation makes it very difficult to justify the use of a workaround internally, or to plan for when the standard function will be available.

Our Questions:

  1. Is there an official SAP Note or KBA that confirms the current unavailability of the "Post Revaluation" function in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition? If so, could you please share the Note/KBA number?
  2. Is "Other Transactions" the officially supported and recommended workaround for revaluation posting at this time? Are there any known limitations or caveats when using this approach?
  3. Is there a planned release timeline for when "Post Revaluation" will become fully available? Which release version is it targeted for?
  4. Why is this limitation not documented in SAP Help Portal or other official SAP publications? If this is a Cloud-specific restriction, should it not be clearly stated in the product documentation?

We would appreciate a clear and direct response to each of the above questions, as we need official written confirmation to proceed with our internal processes and planning.

Thank you for your support.

#FI-AA
##Fixed Asset Accountin
#asset-revaluatio
#post-revaluatio
#fixed-assets

entry.title: Customer Unable to Raise Support Ticket
entry.content:
One of our SAP customers' is unable to to raise support ticket.
 

CUSTOMER INFO:
-----------------------------


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Customer Unable to Raise Support Ticket
entry.content:
I understand your end customer, * is unable to create cases in SAP for Me. I reviewed our backend and do not see a valid contract associated with this customer account. The current status shows: Customer is without Maintenance and can´t use SAP for Me. I have attached a reference so you can see the same status I am seeing. 

Regarding to the issue , please kindly contact your Account Manager or SAP Order Management via sapcontractcenter@sap.com:

My product support team can only tell you if your end customer have a valid contract with SAP, we can't fix issues like this. Therefore, please work directly with SAP Order Management (contracts) and/or your SAP Account Manager to check your end customer'saccount and make any necessary updates. After their account is updated and authorized for the product, your end customer will be able to use it.

entry.title: Re: Custom App Interface Publishing Issues of ABAP Develo...
entry.content:
if you want to create active instance directly, you need to send IsActiveEntity=true in your payload for creation.

generally fiori UI never send IsActiveEntity=true, so you can use that to differentiate if the call is from external system or UI.

entry.title: Re: Custom Infotype to the SuccessFactors Mashup - Technical Configuration Guide
entry.content: Very detailed information .Would be very helpful if there is a situation for a Custom infotype in ECP which i have not seen quite often. Thanks @shankar_ramigani

entry.title: Re: Crystal Reports runtime application to Crystal Report...
entry.content: I have developed a VB.NET report runner using Crystal Reports Runtime 13.0.35. But I am having issues specifically with updating the subreports. The runner dynamically changes reports from the design server: DESKTOP-94TBRUD\MSSQLSERVER01 to the runtime server: BRPSQL1 using RAS ReplaceConnection() and additional DataSourceConnection.SetConnection() calls. Facts established: 1. Main report connection successfully changes to BRPSQL1. 2. Embedded subreport "Boning Room Input.rpt" contains 3 SQL tables: * rptYieldCarcaseDetails * ProductMaster * ProductDescriptions 3. Runtime logging confirms: * subDoc.DataSourceConnections(0).SetConnection(BRPSQL1, Meat_Plant, ...) * DataSourceConnections(0).ServerName = BRPSQL1 * DataSourceConnections(0).DatabaseName = Meat_Plant 4. Runtime logging also confirms all subreport tables are updated: VERIFY SERVER = BRPSQL1 VERIFY DATABASE = Meat_Plant for all three tables. 5. Crystal RAS reports: OLD CONNECTION COUNT = 1 6. Despite all logged connections showing BRPSQL1, the viewer still behaves as follows: * First login prompt = BRPSQL1 * Credentials entered * Second login prompt = DESKTOP-94TBRUD\MSSQLSERVER01 * Logon Failed 7. If the Boning Room Input subreport is removed from the main report, the second login prompt disappears. 8. The subreport is NOT an on-demand subreport. 9. Database Expert shows standard SQL tables under: DESKTOP-94TBRUD\MSSQLSERVER01 Question: Has anyone seen a Crystal subreport continue prompting for the original design-time server after: * ReplaceConnection() * DataSourceConnections.SetConnection() * Table ApplyLogOnInfo() all report success and all table/server verification logging shows the new server? What other Crystal objects can retain a saved datasource/login independently of the tables and DataSourceConnections collection?

entry.title: Assistance to Remove discontinued System in the SAP Support Portal
entry.content:
Hello,

In SAP Support Portal, all sections have lot of implementation which are discontinued or decommissioned few years ago.

We like SAPs help, a working session to streamline the portal as well as contractual entities in the Support Portal.

Attached sheet has details of the Systems currently in Use and other system/implementation should be removed.  

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Assistance to Remove discontinued System in the SAP S...
entry.content: Regarding your request, please refer to KBA 1843303 - How to delete On-premise systems - SAP for Me
